title: StarkWare Outlines Post-Quantum Cryptographic Roadmap for Starknet Architecture - Quantum Computing Report
gist: StarkWare is executing a phased post-quantum migration for Starknet, arguing its STARK/hash-based proof system is natively quantum-resistant while its ECC-dependent bridge and data-availability layers must wait on Ethereum's own PQ timeline.

insights.claim: Starknet's STARK proof system relies on collision-resistant hash functions rather than elliptic-curve arithmetic, making its core proving layer natively insulated from the quantum vulnerabilities that threaten other blockchains' settlement infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Positions Starknet as a structurally advantaged L2 in a post-quantum world versus ECC-reliant chains; a differentiator to watch as PQ risk gets priced.
insights.quote: its underlying proving layer is natively insulated from the specific quantum vulnerabilities that affect the settlement infrastructures of alternative blockchain networks

insights.claim: Starknet's native account abstraction lets quantum-resistant signature schemes be deployed at the contract layer without breaking protocol changes at the core consensus layer.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Lower upgrade friction than chains requiring hard forks; reduces execution risk on the PQ transition.
insights.quote: native account abstraction, which enables the implementation of quantum-resistant signature schemes at the contract layer without necessitating breaking protocol changes

insights.claim: Phase 1 replaces Pedersen hashing (which inherits elliptic-curve assumptions) with BLAKE2 across the state commitment trie, contract address derivation, and OS configuration hash layers, with the OS config hash update slated for mainnet in early July 2026.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Concrete near-term milestone; early July 2026 mainnet deployment is a checkable execution signal.
insights.quote: replacing Pedersen hashing—which inherits elliptic-curve assumptions—with BLAKE2 across the state commitment trie

insights.claim: OpenZeppelin is developing Falcon-512 post-quantum signature options for integration into Starknet account contracts.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals which PQ signature standard is being adopted in the ecosystem; OpenZeppelin involvement lends credibility.
insights.quote: the upcoming integration of Falcon-512 post-quantum signature options into account contracts currently being developed by OpenZeppelin

insights.claim: Phase 2 introduces automated native migration toolkits letting developers upgrade legacy storage slot key derivations without manual data migration or contract interface disruption.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Lower migration cost for existing deployments reduces ecosystem churn risk during the transition.

insights.claim: Starknet's L1-to-L2 bridge messaging relies on quantum-vulnerable secp256k1/r1 syscalls and its data-availability layer uses ECC-based KZG commitments inside EIP-4844 blobs, so these fixes are gated on Ethereum's own upstream post-quantum upgrades.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Starknet's full PQ security is bottlenecked by Ethereum's slower timeline; the L2's quantum readiness cannot outrun L1.
insights.quote: The removal of these specific syscalls and the optimization of blob commitments will be executed conditionally following Ethereum’s upstream cryptographic upgrades
